angle: Why new health routines fail in the first three weeks — and how to fix it
summary: Most people abandon health routines not from lack of motivation but from lack of body literacy. Learning to read your own body's signals — sleep quality, energy patterns, hunger cues — is the skill that makes any routine sustainable.
definition: Health Intelligence (HQ) is the ability to accurately read your own health status, seek relevant information, and translate it into self-care decisions. It sits alongside IQ and EQ as a third measurable intelligence.

key_facts.fact: People who track even one health metric daily are 2.4× more likely to maintain a new health habit after 90 days
73% of people who start a new health routine quit within 3 weeks
key_facts.source: Behaviour Change meta-analysis, University of Melbourne, 2022

key_facts.fact: Sleep quality, not duration, is the strongest predictor of daily energy levels

common_misconception: Most people think health routines fail because of willpower. The real reason is misalignment — copying someone else's routine instead of responding to your own body's signals.
practical_steps: Track your sleep and wake time every day for two weeks — even just writing it down creates awareness
Pick the one health metric that feels most out of control and focus there first
Notice the patterns in your own data before adding any new habit

contrasting_approaches.label: Medical management
contrasting_approaches.description: Using medicine or procedures to address specific health issues

contrasting_approaches.label: Evidence-based management
contrasting_approaches.description: Using personal data and research-backed habits to improve daily wellbeing

contrasting_approaches.label: Holistic management
contrasting_approaches.description: Looking at the whole person — body, environment, relationships — as an interconnected system

hook: You don't need more willpower. You need to learn the language your body is already speaking.
do_examples: Observe and record your own patterns first
Use simple tools — a note on your phone is enough
dont_examples: Copy someone else's routine without adapting it
Chase perfection and quit when you miss a day
Rely on complex apps that create more friction than insight
